attention1.ptxt: Steering lock is made of magnesium.
Drilling, cutting or filing the steering lock may spark to ignite magnesium powder, causing a fire or burn.
Do not perform drilling, cutting or filing magnesium powder on the steering lock.

attention4.ptxt: Failure to take proper precautions when removing or installing the steering lock.

item.ptxt: Do not drop or otherwise expose the steering lock to large shock. Any the steering lock that has received large shock should be replaced.
Keep the steering column and/or the steering lock free of oil, water, dust or other foreign materials.

attention3.ptxt: The following removal procedure for steering lock is also applicable to auto steering lock.

s1.ptxt: Loosen steering lock bolts (2) using chisel, then remove auto steering lock (3) from steering column (1).
Install auto steering lock (1) to steering shaft (2) with the column cover mounting screw hole (3) facing as shown in figure.
Tighten new steering lock bolts (1) until head of each bolt is broken off.
Set ignition ACC or ON and check that steering shaft rotates smoothly. Then set ignition OFF and check that steering wheel is locked.
Check steering column and torque sensor after installation. Replace steering column if defective.
